California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
May 18,
1993
Mr.
Thomas S.
Knox
Knox,
Leinmon, Lrdy,
et al.
300 Capitol Mall,
Suite
1170
Sacramento,
CA 95814
Re:
Your Request For Confirmation
of Advice
Our File No.
1—93—160
Dear Mr.
Knox:
You have requested confirmation of advice provided to you
concerning the conflict of interest disclosure provisions of the
Political reform Act. 1/
Since your request is one for general
information,
we are treating yor request as one for informal
assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).2./
Your letter,
dated April
14,
1993, correctly summarizes our
advice conr2rning the statements of economic interests required to
be filed by Oaiifornia-Nevada Super Speed Gro’ind Transportation
Commissioners and designated employees.
1/
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et g.
All references to regulations are to Title
2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2/
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114;
2 Cal.
Code of Regs.
Section
18329(c) (3) .)
42S j Street, Suite 800
•
P.O. Box 807
•
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807
•
(916) 32Z-566()


Mr.
Thomas S.
Knox
1—93—160
Page Two
In addition to Frank Michelena’s Leaving Office statement
(pending FPPC advice), it appears that we have not received the
following statements:
1.
Arthur Pick --Confined Assuming Office and Annual
Statements
2.
Thomas McKnew
-— Leaving Office Statement
3.
Tom Skanke
-- Assuming Office Statement
If the officials listed above do not file with you by May 31,
1993, please contact iue so that the Commission can notify th.iñ
directly regarding their obligations to file pursuant to the
Political Reform Act.
Please contact me at
(916)
322-5662 if you have any questions
regarding this matter.
.
Sincerely,.
I&2zJ
Alice Hughes
Political Reform Consultant

sections.question_synthetic: The requester sought confirmation of previously-provided FPPC advice regarding conflict of interest disclosure requirements for California-Nevada Super Speed Ground Transportation Commissioners and designated employees.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirmed that the requester's April 14, 1993 letter correctly summarized the advice concerning statements of economic interests. The FPPC also identified missing required filings from specific officials and requested notification if those officials did not file by May 31, 1993.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a confirmation/informal advice letter without traditional Q/C sections. Document contains references to specific missing statements and a deadline for filing compliance. Some OCR errors present in original text (e.g., 'Gro'ind', 'th.iñ', 'iue').

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requester sought confirmation of previously-provided FPPC advice regarding conflict of interest disclosure requirements for California-Nevada Super Speed Ground Transportation Commissioners and designated employees.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC confirmed that the requester's April 14, 1993 letter correctly summarized the advice concerning statements of economic interests. The FPPC also identified missing required filings from specific officials and requested notification if those officials did not file by May 31, 1993.

embedding.summary: The FPPC confirms previously-provided advice regarding economic interest disclosure requirements for California-Nevada Super Speed Ground Transportation officials and identifies outstanding required filings that must be submitted by May 31, 1993.
